sanskrit: जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः । त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥
hindiTranslation: हे अर्जुन  मेरे जन्म और कर्म दिव्य हैं ।  इस प्रकार मेरे जन्म और कर्मको जो मनुष्य तत्त्वसे जान लेता अर्थात् दृढ़तापूर्वक मान लेता है, वह शरीरका त्याग करके पुनर्जन्मको प्राप्त नहीं होता, प्रत्युत मुझे प्राप्त होता है ॥
englishTranslation: Arjuna, my birth and action are divine. One who truly understands this does not take birth again after leaving the body, but comes to me.
essence: Seeing Krishna truly ends the cycle of return.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says his birth and actions are not ordinary. Whoever understands their divine nature reaches him and is not born again.
simpleInsight: There is relief in seeing that the deepest reality is already working through what looks human.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still frozen, and Krishna is explaining who he really is. After describing why he appears in the world, Krishna says that his birth and action are divine. Understanding that truth changes a person’s fate beyond bodily death.
modernRelevance: You meet someone who seems ordinary, yet their presence changes everything. The mind that sees only the surface misses the deeper reality already shaping the moment.
translationLiteral: जन्म (birth) / कर्म (action) / च (and) / मे (my) / दिव्यम् (divine) / एवम् (thus) / यः (who) / वेत्ति (knows) / तत्त्वतः (in truth) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / देहम् (the body) / पुनर्जन्म (rebirth) / न (not) / एति (goes) / माम् (to me) / एति (goes) / सः (that one) / अर्जुन (Arjuna)
